site.title: AI Builder's Field Guide
site.tagline: A daily-curated wiki of the tools, models, and patterns that AI power-users and builders actually reach for.

categories.name: Coding Agents & IDEs
categories.blurb: Agents that read, write, and run your code.

categories.name: Foundation Models & APIs
categories.blurb: The frontier models you call from your stack.

categories.name: Agent Frameworks & SDKs
categories.blurb: Glue for building autonomous, tool-using agents.

categories.name: Evals & Observability
categories.blurb: Measure what your agents actually do in production.

categories.name: App & UI Builders
categories.blurb: Generate working apps from a prompt.

categories.name: Voice & Multimodal
categories.blurb: Speech, vision, and real-time multimodal stacks.

categories.name: MCP Ecosystem
categories.blurb: Servers, clients, and registries for the Model Context Protocol.

entries.name: Claude Code
entries.summary: Anthropic's official terminal-native coding agent — reads your repo, edits files, runs commands, talks to MCP servers.
entries.why_it_matters: The reference implementation for agentic coding: full-context repo understanding, permissions model, hooks, and skills. If you're building developer agents, study how it ships.

entries.name: Cursor
entries.summary: AI-first VS Code fork with Composer, Tab autocomplete, and a strong agent loop.
entries.why_it_matters: The dominant IDE among working AI builders in 2026 — diff review UX is the bar everyone else is chasing.

entries.name: Windsurf
entries.summary: Codeium's agentic IDE with Cascade — strong long-running task execution, now part of OpenAI.
entries.why_it_matters: Cascade pioneered the 'agent that keeps going across many turns' pattern; worth comparing against Cursor Composer for taste.

entries.name: Aider
entries.summary: Open-source CLI pair-programmer — git-native, model-agnostic, and the benchmark for many leaderboards.
entries.why_it_matters: The polyglot baseline. If you're benchmarking your own agent, Aider's leaderboards are where the field calibrates.

entries.name: Cline
entries.summary: Open-source autonomous coding agent for VS Code — plan/act modes, MCP support, BYO model.
entries.why_it_matters: The transparent option: every action shown and approvable. Good for understanding how an agent loop actually works.

entries.name: Claude API (Anthropic)
entries.summary: Anthropic's API — Claude Opus 4.7, Sonnet 4.6, Haiku 4.5. Native tool use, prompt caching, extended thinking, files, citations.
entries.why_it_matters: Best-in-class for long-horizon tool use and agentic coding. Prompt caching alone often pays for the migration.

entries.name: OpenAI API
entries.summary: GPT-5 family, Realtime API, structured outputs, Responses API.
entries.why_it_matters: Still the broadest ecosystem and the realtime voice baseline. Responses API is the cleanest agent primitive OpenAI ships.

entries.name: Gemini API (Google)
entries.summary: Gemini 2.5 Pro / Flash with 2M-token context, native multimodal, and grounding-with-Search.
entries.why_it_matters: If your workload is video, very-long-context, or needs fresh web grounding without your own RAG, start here.

entries.name: OpenRouter
entries.summary: Single API for hundreds of models with automatic failover and unified billing.
entries.why_it_matters: The pragmatic abstraction layer when you don't want to hard-code one provider. Useful for model A/B and graceful fallback.

entries.name: Claude Agent SDK
entries.summary: Anthropic's official SDK for building agents that share Claude Code's runtime — tools, MCP, hooks, subagents.
entries.why_it_matters: If you want Claude Code's agent harness for your own product, this is the supported path. Less reinvention than building from raw API calls.

entries.name: LangGraph
entries.summary: Graph-based agent orchestration from LangChain — durable execution, human-in-the-loop, time-travel debugging.
entries.why_it_matters: When your agent has real branching state and you need to checkpoint/replay, the graph model pays off.

entries.name: Mastra
entries.summary: TypeScript-first agent framework — workflows, memory, evals, and a local dev playground.
entries.why_it_matters: The most ergonomic TS option in 2026 for builders who don't want to leave Node. Strong eval story baked in.

entries.name: Inspect (UK AISI)
entries.summary: Open-source eval framework from the UK AI Safety Institute — used for the public capability evals you read about.
entries.why_it_matters: If you're publishing eval results you want anyone to reproduce, Inspect is the lingua franca.

entries.name: Braintrust
entries.summary: Eval, logging, and prompt-playground stack used by many production AI teams.
entries.why_it_matters: The 'we ship LLM features and need to know if a prompt change broke things' tool — diffs across runs are first-class.

entries.name: Langfuse
entries.summary: Open-source LLM observability — traces, evals, prompts, self-hostable.
entries.why_it_matters: The default open-source pick for tracing agent runs. Self-host if your data can't leave the building.

entries.name: pgvector
entries.summary: Vector similarity search inside Postgres. HNSW + IVFFlat indexes, just another column type.
entries.why_it_matters: The boring-tech RAG stack: keep your embeddings next to your relational data. Most apps don't need a dedicated vector DB.

entries.name: turbopuffer
entries.summary: Serverless vector + full-text search on object storage — pay per query, scales to billions of vectors.
entries.why_it_matters: When pgvector starts groaning and you don't want to babysit a Pinecone bill, turbopuffer is the 2026 sweet spot.

entries.name: v0 by Vercel
entries.summary: Generate React/Next.js UIs from prompts — full-stack mode, git sync, deploy to Vercel in one click.
entries.why_it_matters: Highest-fidelity prompt-to-production UI in 2026 if your stack is Next + shadcn. Treat it as a fast scaffolder, not a finisher.

entries.name: Lovable
entries.summary: Prompt-to-app builder — full-stack, Supabase-backed, GitHub-synced.
entries.why_it_matters: The leading non-developer-friendly app builder. Good for testing whether an idea has shape before you spend a dev week on it.

entries.name: Bolt.new
entries.summary: StackBlitz's in-browser full-stack agent — runs Node in WebContainers, no local setup.
entries.why_it_matters: When the friction of 'clone, install, run' is the bottleneck on idea velocity, Bolt removes it entirely.

entries.name: ElevenLabs
entries.summary: TTS, voice cloning, conversational AI, and the Agents platform for realtime voice.
entries.why_it_matters: Still the quality bar for synthetic voice. The Agents platform is the fastest path from idea to a phone-callable voice agent.

entries.name: Cartesia
entries.summary: Sonic — sub-100ms streaming TTS with state-space models. The realtime-voice latency leader.
entries.why_it_matters: If your voice agent feels laggy, the model is usually the issue. Cartesia's the default fix.

entries.name: Model Context Protocol
entries.summary: Open standard for connecting LLMs to tools, data, and prompts. Now supported by Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and most agent frameworks.
entries.why_it_matters: MCP won the 'how do agents talk to tools' debate. If you're building anything an agent needs to use, ship an MCP server.

entries.name: Smithery
entries.summary: Registry and one-click installer for MCP servers — discover, install, and host MCP servers without local config.
entries.why_it_matters: The npm of the MCP ecosystem in 2026. Start here when you need an MCP server before writing one.

entries.name: MemPalace
entries.summary: Open-source local AI memory system — stores conversation history as verbatim text with semantic retrieval.
entries.why_it_matters: Hits 96.6% R@5 on LongMemEval with zero API calls. If you want agent memory that doesn't leak to a vendor, this is the open-source baseline.

entries.name: OB1
entries.summary: Self-hosted shared memory database — lets multiple AI tools read and write one persistent memory without vendor lock-in.
entries.why_it_matters: Bridges the memory silos: Claude, Cursor, and ChatGPT each have their own. OB1 is the memory backbone that unifies them.

entries.name: You.com Search API
entries.summary: Web search API engineered for AI agents — 300ms p99 latency, real-time indexing across 10M+ news sources.
entries.why_it_matters: When you need fresh web grounding inside an agent and Tavily or Exa feels slow, You.com's latency is the differentiator.

entries.name: DeepResearch-Bench
entries.summary: Open benchmark and leaderboard for deep-research agents (Claude, OpenAI, Gemini, and OSS) — hosted by muset-ai on Hugging Face Spaces.
entries.why_it_matters: If you're shipping a research agent or deciding which deep-research product to integrate, this is the public scoreboard to read first.

entries.name: Zed AI
entries.summary: Rust-native code editor with agentic editing, real-time collaboration, and the open Agent Client Protocol (ACP).
entries.why_it_matters: The performance-first alternative to Cursor and Windsurf. 120 fps render, open prediction model (Zeta), and ACP make agent behavior auditable.

entries.name: Conductor
entries.summary: Mac app that runs a fleet of Claude Code and Codex agents in parallel git worktrees, then helps you review and merge their work.
entries.why_it_matters: Single-machine multi-agent orchestration with native macOS UX. The 'one developer, many parallel agents' workflow distilled into a desktop app.

entries.name: Paseo
entries.summary: Unified runner for Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, and others — accessible from phone, desktop, or terminal.
entries.why_it_matters: If you switch between coding agents and devices, Paseo is the connector. Picks up where Claude.ai's mobile leaves off for power users.

entries.name: Sandcastle
entries.summary: TypeScript library by Matt Pocock for orchestrating AI coding agents in isolated sandboxes — one function call handles lifecycle and branch merging.
entries.why_it_matters: If you're building a product that spawns coding agents, Sandcastle removes the boilerplate around containers and merge UX.

entries.name: Taalas
entries.summary: Compiles AI models directly into custom silicon — model weights and parameters live on the chip, eliminating memory retrieval.
entries.why_it_matters: Their ChatJimmy demo hits ~17K tokens/sec/user vs. ~1,800 on GPUs. Hardware bet that 'the model is the computer' — worth watching if inference cost dominates your stack.

entries.name: HyperFrames
entries.summary: HeyGen's open-source HTML-based video rendering framework with first-class AI agent support (Apache 2.0).
entries.why_it_matters: Remotion needs React and a build step; HyperFrames lets an agent author videos as plain HTML. Better fit for LLM agents that already speak HTML fluently.

entries.name: Remotion
entries.summary: React-based framework for creating videos programmatically using CSS, Canvas, SVG, and WebGL.
entries.why_it_matters: The most mature programmatic-video stack. Treat video as a React app, render in CI. Source-available — commercial use needs a license.

entries.name: Tripo3D
entries.summary: Text, image, or sketch to production-ready 3D assets — texturing, rigging, and animation in one workflow.
entries.why_it_matters: The all-in-one 3D generator for game, AR, and product pipelines. Beats single-feature competitors when you need riggable output, not just a mesh.

entries.name: Hunyuan3D-2
entries.summary: Tencent's open 3D asset generator — two-stage pipeline with flow-based diffusion transformer for shape and separate texture synthesis.
entries.why_it_matters: Open weights for high-resolution textured 3D. Can also re-texture handcrafted meshes — useful when Tripo3D's hosted workflow isn't an option.

entries.name: Impeccable
entries.summary: Design skill for Claude Code, Cursor, and Gemini CLI — 23 commands to push AI-generated UIs past generic defaults.
entries.why_it_matters: Most AI-built UIs look the same because the agents lack design vocabulary. Impeccable installs that vocabulary as a reusable skill.

entries.name: Claude Design System Prompt
entries.summary: Public gist of a Claude system prompt for design and prototyping — HTML artifacts, design systems, animation, asset verification.
entries.why_it_matters: A worked example of turning a coding agent into a design partner. Steal the structure for your own design skill or compare to Impeccable.
